prompt: I have a contract at /documents/vendor-agreement.pdf. Can you open it and highlight the key terms I should pay attention to before signing?
expected_output: Agent calls display_pdf with the file path, then uses get_text to read the contract, proposes a set of annotations (highlights, notes) to the user describing what it will mark, then on implicit approval applies them via interact add_annotations and confirms with get_screenshot. Ends by reminding the user they can download the annotated PDF.

assertions.description: Calls display_pdf with the file path
assertions.check: The response describes calling display_pdf (or equivalent) with the vendor-agreement.pdf path

assertions.description: Reads document content before proposing annotations
assertions.check: The response shows the agent using get_text or equivalent to understand the contract content before deciding what to highlight

assertions.description: Proposes annotation plan to user before applying
assertions.check: The agent describes to the user what it plans to highlight or annotate and invites approval or adjustment

assertions.description: Uses get_screenshot to confirm annotation result
assertions.check: The agent calls get_screenshot after applying annotations to visually verify and show the result to the user
The response mentions that the user can download the annotated PDF from the viewer toolbar

prompt: Help me fill out the W-9 tax form at /forms/w9.pdf. I need to put it in as John Smith, sole proprietor, SSN 555-12-3456, address 100 Main St, Springfield, IL.
expected_output: Agent calls display_pdf on the W-9, inspects the returned formFields (which on a W-9 have cryptic names like f1_1, f1_2), takes a screenshot to match cryptic names to visual labels, then fills the fields using fill_form with the user-provided values, takes a screenshot to confirm, and shows the result. Notes that the signature line still needs a signature image.

assertions.description: Calls display_pdf to open the form
assertions.check: The agent calls display_pdf on the W-9 path

assertions.description: Takes screenshot to resolve cryptic field names
assertions.check: Because W-9 fields have cryptic names (f1_1 etc.), the agent uses get_screenshot to visually identify which field is which before filling
The agent fills in all values provided: name (John Smith), business type/SSN, and address using fill_form
The agent calls get_screenshot after fill_form to show the user the filled result

assertions.description: Notes signature field still needs signature image
assertions.check: The agent points out that the signature line requires a separate signature image step (visual sign workflow)

prompt: I need to sign the NDA at ~/Documents/nda-2024.pdf on page 4. My signature image is at ~/Desktop/signature.png.
expected_output: Agent calls display_pdf on the NDA, checks formFields for signature-type fields or asks about placement on page 4. Places the signature image using interact add_annotations with type 'image', imageUrl pointing to the .png, on page 4 at appropriate coordinates (e.g., bottom area). Calls get_screenshot of page 4 to confirm placement. Offers to adjust if positioning looks off. Includes the disclaimer about this being a visual signature, not a certified digital signature.

assertions.check: The agent calls display_pdf on the NDA path
The agent uses an image annotation type with imageUrl pointing to the signature.png file to place the signature
The image annotation is placed on page 4 as specified by the user
The agent calls get_screenshot of page 4 after placing the annotation to verify and show the user
The response includes a disclaimer that this is a visual signature image, not a certified or cryptographic digital signature

prompt: Can you summarize the key points in /reports/annual-review-2023.pdf for me?
expected_output: Agent correctly identifies this as a pure text extraction / summarization task — NOT an interactive viewer task. It uses the native Read tool to read the PDF content directly and returns a summary. It does NOT call display_pdf or open the viewer at all.

assertions.description: Does NOT open the PDF viewer
assertions.check: The agent does not call display_pdf or open an interactive viewer for this summarization request
The agent uses the native Read tool or equivalent text extraction to access the PDF content for summarization
The response provides a substantive summary of what the annual review would contain, demonstrating correct routing

prompt: I'm reviewing a research paper at https://arxiv.org/abs/2401.01234. Walk me through it and annotate the methodology and results sections with your analysis notes.
expected_output: Agent opens the arXiv paper (auto-converting the /abs/ URL to the PDF URL) via display_pdf, uses get_text to read through the paper in sections, proposes annotations for the methodology and results sections (notes, highlights on key findings), applies them in batches with get_screenshot after each batch, and iterates section by section. Reminds user they can download at the end.

assertions.check: The agent opens the arXiv paper — either noting it auto-converts /abs/ URLs to PDF URLs or passing the URL to display_pdf
The agent uses get_text to read methodology and/or results sections before proposing annotations
The agent batches annotations in manageable groups (not all at once) and shows the user each batch
get_screenshot is called after each batch of add_annotations to verify and present results
The response mentions downloading the annotated PDF from the viewer toolbar
